name: Kahuku Superette
tagline: A five-generation family recipe in a tiny convenience store on the North Shore — the shoyu poke here will ruin you for all others.
mustOrder: Shoyu ahi poke bowl — five-generation family recipe, the reason this place exists
Spicy ahi poke — creamy-spicy with fresh ahi
Plate lunch specials — surprisingly solid comfort food from a convenience store
parking: Small gravel lot right in front of the store
talkStory: A five-generation family-owned country store in Kahuku town — the Tanioka family has been making poke here with the same recipe since the plantation era, making it one of the oldest continuously family-made poke preparations on Oahu. It looks like a convenience store because it is one, but the poke counter in the back is the real reason people drive out here. The shoyu ahi poke is the signature — simple, clean, and perfect. Go before noon on weekends because it sells out. Pair with a Kahuku shrimp truck stop nearby.
